Document 2: The naval Battle of Salamis in 306 BC took place near Salamis, Cyprus between the fleets of Ptolemy I of Egypt and Antigonus I Monophthalmus.

Document 3: The Rebellion of Cao Qin was a day-long uprising in the Ming Dynasty capital of Beijing staged by Chinese general Cao Qin and his Ming troops.

Document 2: There are several solar power plants in the Mojave Desert which supply power to the electricity grid.
These plants can generally be built in a few years because solar plants are built almost entirely with modular, readily available materials.

topic.name: Pop Music Icons and Tours
topic.description: The documents discuss the careers, notable songs, and concert tours of prominent pop music artists, specifically Britney Spears and Madonna.

Document 1: The Femme Fatale Tour was the eighth concert tour by American recording artist Britney Spears
Spears has named the Femme Fatale Tour the best show of her career.
Document 2: 'Toxic' is a song recorded by American singer Britney Spears for her fourth studio album
The song has received critical acclaim by music critics.
Document 3: 'Like a Prayer' is a song by American singer Madonna
It was acclaimed by critics, and was a commercial success.

topic.name: Australian Cricket History
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant events, players, and achievements in the history of Australian cricket.

Document 1: Warwick Armstrong was an Australian cricketer who played 50 Test matches between 1902 and 1921.
Armstrong was captain of the 1920 – 21 Australian team which defeated the touring English 5 – 0.
Document 2: The Australian cricket team in England in 1948 was captained by Don Bradman, who was making his fourth and final tour of England.
The team is famous for being the first Test match side to play an entire tour of England without losing a match.

topic.name: Australian and New Zealand Military Participation in WWI
topic.description: The documents discuss the involvement of Australian and New Zealand forces in World War I, highlighting key campaigns and military formations.

1: Australia pledged its support alongside other states of the British Empire
the first campaign that Australians were involved in was in German New Guinea
2: Wellington Mounted Rifles Regiment was raised for service during the First World War
they participated in the largest battle of that theatre at Chunuk Bair
3: the 2nd Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army
it saw action at Gallipoli before being sent to the Western Front
